reasoning: A 50% decline in four of the seven largest US stocks requires a severe and highly correlated market crash. Such events (e.g., 2000, 2008, 2022) occur roughly once per decade. Although Tesla is already >50% below its November 2021 ATH (as of early 2024), requiring three more mega-cap tech stocks to halve in value by the end of 2025 is a steep hurdle. The AI boom could experience a correction, but a >50% simultaneous drop across four names in a ~1.5 to 2-year window remains unlikely absent a major macroeconomic shock. Therefore, my probability estimate is low.
